title: Upstream podman loses the hostname when reading the ssh_config
body:
### Issue Description

I was testing to see if the upstream version of Podman can read the ssh config, and it does but the host entry in the ssh config makes the connection lose the hostname and gives an error when connecting

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Set an entry in the ssh config like

```
cat ~/.ssh/config
host uav30
	hostname uav30
	user mrs
	identityfile ~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

Run podman and observe that the DEBUG info says the ssh_config was found

```
./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url="ssh://mrs@uav30:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock" version
INFO[0000] ./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 filtering at log level debug 
DEBU[0000] Called version.PersistentPreRunE(./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url=ssh://mrs@uav30:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock version) 
DEBU[0000] ssh_config alias found: uav30                
DEBU[0000]   User: mrs                                  
DEBU[0000]   Hostname: uav30                            
DEBU[0000]   Port: 22                                   
DEBU[0000]   IdentityFile: "/home/mrs/.ssh/id_rsa"      
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/home/mrs/.ssh/id_rsa" SHA256:YQvkeUiU2Mw2Br16looSPEIDeaUgp9lN1GcN1zvEZHs ssh-rsa 
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp :22: connect: connection refused
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

### Describe the results you received

the hostname is somehow lost after the ssh config was read

```
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp :22: connect: connection refused
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the command to behave the same as if I didn't have anything set in my ssh config

```
cat ~/.ssh/config 
#host uav30
#	hostname uav30
#	user mrs
#	identityfile ~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

And now running the same command works

```
./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url="ssh://mrs@uav30:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock" version
INFO[0000] ./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 filtering at log level debug 
DEBU[0000] Called version.PersistentPreRunE(./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url=ssh://mrs@uav30:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock version) 
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/run/user/1000/keyring/ssh", ssh-agent signer enabled 
DEBU[0000] SSH Agent Key SHA256:YQvkeUiU2Mw2Br16looSPEIDeaUgp9lN1GcN1zvEZHs ssh-rsa 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.6.1/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.6.1/libpod/version 
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.6.1
API Version:  5.6.1
Go Version:   go1.25.1
Git Commit:   1e2b2315150b2ffa0971596fb5da8cd83f3ce0e1
Built:        Thu Sep  4 22:51:41 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.9.3
API Version:  4.9.3
Go Version:   go1.22.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
DEBU[0001] Called version.PersistentPostRunE(./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url=ssh://mrs@uav30:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock version) 
DEBU[0001] Shutting down engines
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I don't think this is how we designed this to work, but it is interesting.  @mheon this is a feature request to use this without system connections or ?

Ideally I'd like it to read the ssh config so that only "uav30" is provided in the command with no username🤷‍♂️in the current version on Ubuntu 24.04, the username isn't even set to the current user though which is why I went to see what the new version does
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Seems like this ought to work, so not a feature request.

A similar issue has already been fixed. See https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/25067
I guess we can close this

> A similar issue has already been fixed. See [#25067](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/25067) I guess we can close this

So I can retry it with [v6.0.1](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/releases/tag/v6.0.1)? and then I'll let you know if I find any ssh more ssh_config inconsistencies

@jiwahn I confirm that in a similar scenario as originally described, the ssh options from `~/.ssh/config` were correctly loaded by podman [v6.0.1](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/releases/tag/v6.0.1). I tested User, Hostname, Port and IdentityFile
```bash
mrs@FilipPad:~/Desktop$ ./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url="ssh://mrs-HP-Z2/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock" version
INFO[0000] ./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 filtering at log level debug 
DEBU[0000] Called version.PersistentPreRunE(./podman-remote-static-linux_amd64 --log-level=debug --url=ssh://mrs-HP-Z2/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock version) 
DEBU[0000] ssh_config alias found: mrs-HP-Z2            
DEBU[0000]   User: mrs                                  
DEBU[0000]   Hostname: ssh://192.168.88.57              
DEBU[0000]   Port: 22                                   
DEBU[0000]   IdentityFile: "/home/mrs/.ssh/id_rsa"      
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/home/mrs/.ssh/id_rsa" SHA256:YQvkeUiU2Mw2Br16looSPEIDeaUgp9lN1GcN1zvEZHs ssh-rsa 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v6.0.1/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v6.0.1/libpod/version
```
